name: email
description: Handle email operations like checking, searching, and summarizing unread messages using himalaya.

control_plane_triggers.regex: ^(check mail|read mail|read email|check email|inbox|did i get any mail)[.!?\s]*$

tools.name: list_unread
tools.description: List the last N unread emails.
examples: do i have any unread messages

tools.name: summarize_email
tools.description: Fetch, read, or summarize the content of a specific email. Call this immediately when the user wants to read an email. Pass the sender name or subject words as subject_query — this is the primary path.
examples: tell me what the invite says

email_id.description: A numeric email ID. Use this when you have a specific numeric ID. One ID per call — to read multiple emails, call this tool once per ID.

subject_query.description: Keywords from the email subject or sender name to find the email. Use this when you only have a description (e.g. 'email from Sarah').

tools.name: configure_email
tools.description: Configure the email skill (Himalaya) with IMAP/SMTP credentials. ALWAYS recommend using an App Password for security.

password.description: The App Password for the email account.

tools.name: reply_email
tools.description: Reply (or reply-all) to an existing email. Automatically fetches sender address, subject (Re: prefix), and message_id for threading. Use when the user says 'reply', 'respond', 'write back', or 'reply all'. Prefer email_id (from a previous list or search) over subject_query.
examples: reply to that email and say yes
write back and tell them i'm available
reply all with the attached document

email_id.description: The email ID (from list_unread or search_emails). Use this when available — more reliable than subject_query.

subject_query.description: Keywords to identify the original email (e.g. 'SXSW invite'). Fallback when email_id is not available.

reply_all.description: If true, CC all original recipients (To + CC) in addition to the sender. Use when user says 'reply all'. Default: false.

tools.name: list_drafts
tools.description: List all pending (unsent) email drafts saved in the Ledger. Use this when the user asks 'do I have any drafts?', 'show me my drafts', 'any emails in draft?', or similar.

tools.name: send_email
tools.description: Send an email to a recipient, optionally with a file attachment. Requires to, subject, and body. If the user uploaded a file (resume, PDF, etc.) and the attachment_path was provided in context, include it.
examples: shoot an email to the team

attachment_path.description: Absolute path to a file on the server to attach. Provided automatically when user uploads a file via Telegram.

tools.name: discard_draft
tools.description: Discard a pending email draft by its ID. Use this when the user says 'forget that draft', 'delete the draft', or 'discard it'.

draft_id.description: The unique ID of the draft to discard (e.g. from list_drafts output).

tools.name: draft_email
tools.description: Compose a draft email and save it for later. Only 'body' is required — 'to' and 'subject' can be filled in later. Use this when the user says 'draft', 'compose', 'jot down', or 'write an email'. Does NOT send. Use send_email to send.
examples: draft an email to the board

to.description: Recipient email address. Optional at draft time — can be added before sending.

subject.description: Email subject line. Optional at draft time — can be added before sending.

draft_id.description: If revising an existing draft, pass the draft_id from the previous draft_email response to update it in-place.

attachment_path.description: Absolute path to a file to attach.

tools.name: search_emails
tools.description: Search the mailbox by proposing candidate keywords. Python handles query construction and retrieval planning — do NOT use OR, AND, or any query syntax. Just provide keyword lists.

subject_keywords.description: Subject terms to match. ANY keyword matching returns the email. Use specific phrases: ['product manager', 'ad tech'] not ['product manager OR ad tech'].

body_keywords.description: Body terms to match. ANY keyword matching returns the email. Use sparingly — subject and sender searches are much faster.

from_keywords.description: Sender names, addresses, or domains. ANY keyword matching returns the email. Example: ['LinkedIn', 'recruiter@apple.com'].

relative_time.description: Time window for the search. Supported: 'today', 'yesterday', '1w_ago'...'6w_ago', '1mo_ago'...'6mo_ago', '1yr_ago'.

examples: Find job emails from Apple or Amazon in the last 2 months → subject_keywords: ['Apple', 'Amazon'], relative_time: '2mo_ago'
Emails from LinkedIn about product manager roles → subject_keywords: ['product manager'], from_keywords: ['LinkedIn'], relative_time: '1mo_ago'
Recent emails mentioning NDA → body_keywords: ['NDA'], relative_time: '2w_ago'

tools.name: account_info
tools.description: Fetch the configured email addresses for this Bregger instance. Use this if you need to know 'who am I?' or 'what is my email address?' before replying to or drafting an email.
